IMP-A.name: File Paths

warnings.detail: PART2 adds rpc/ directory not in PHASE_B2 canonical structure. PART2 self-annotates this as XREF-V0-12 functional addition.
PART2 adds data/ at root (XREF-V0-10). PHASE_B2 has data/ under config/ layout (B4 §1.3). Acceptable divergence.
PART2 adds logs/ at root. PHASE_B2 places logs under data/logs/. Acceptable divergence.

details.description: All file paths in PART2 (src/, src-tauri/, backend/vamos_core/, shared/, config/, scripts/, docs/, .github/workflows/) exist in PHASE_B2 project structure. PART2 references orange_core/, blue_nodes/, infra/, agent/, storage/, safety/, schemas/, mcp/ under vamos_core/ — all match B2 §5.1-5.9. Three extensions (rpc/, data/, logs/) are self-documented in PART2 with XREF notes. Module file paths (i01_intent_detector.py through i20_failure_manager.py) use M-7 naming convention under backend/vamos_core/orange_core/ which is consistent with B2 §5.2 orange_core/ directory.

IMP-A.v8_delta: v8 IMP-A was Module Dependency Graph (nodes/edges/cycles), not File Paths. v13 re-scopes IMP-A to File Path verification per task spec. Different verification target.

IMP-B.name: Dependency Order

details.description: PART2 Week 1-2 table (lines 1782-1789) declares dependency chain: I-1(none) -> I-2(I-1) -> I-15(I-2) -> I-5(I-1,I-2,I-15) -> I-8(I-5) -> I-19(I-5). Week 3-4 table (lines 1793-1805) continues correctly. Topological sort confirms no circular dependencies. The R7 rule (line 135) states CORE->COND unidirectional only, reverse forbidden. VAL-003 (line 4745) validates no circular references at build time. All dependency declarations are consistent.

IMP-B.v8_delta: v8 IMP-A contained the dependency graph (17 nodes, 17 edges, 0 cycles, PASS). v13 IMP-B confirms identical structure with 17 modules and 0 cycles. Result unchanged: PASS.

IMP-C.name: Units/Formats

warnings.detail: PART2 uses mixed suffix conventions: timeout_s (seconds) for approval/node timeouts, default_timeout_ms (milliseconds) for core IPC timeout, duration_ms for response envelope, latency_ms for brain adapter. This is consistent with B4 canonical naming (_ms for milliseconds, _s for seconds).
PART2 line 324/500: ttl_sec=86400 with SOURCE_CONFLICT annotation (B4=3600 vs D2.0-06=86400). PART2 correctly applies DESIGN 2.0 > PHASE_B precedence rule, adopting 86400.

units_verified.timeout_s: seconds — used for approval.timeout_s=600, node timeout_s, tool timeout_s (consistent across PART2 and SOTs)
units_verified.timeout_ms: milliseconds — used for core.default_timeout_ms=10000 (B4 canonical, PART2 lines 311/487)
units_verified.cost_float: float — BrainAdapterResponse.cost (no unit suffix, monetary value)

details.format_consistency: All datetime fields use ISO 8601 (datetime type in Pydantic). All IDs use string type with prefix conventions (dec_, req_, trace_). Enums use UPPER_CASE string literals.
details.description: Units and formats in PART2 are internally consistent and match SOT specifications. The _ms/_s suffix convention follows B4 canonical naming. One documented SOURCE_CONFLICT (ttl_sec) is resolved correctly per precedence rules.

IMP-C.v8_delta: v8 IMP-C was API Endpoint Validation (IPC/RPC cross-check, FAIL with 3 error groups). v13 re-scopes IMP-C to Units/Formats consistency. Different verification target; new result is PASS.

IMP-D.name: Timeouts

warnings.detail: PART2 adds S3a=600s (P2 approval wait) not explicitly in D2.0-02 §12.1 base state list (S1-S5 only). However, D2.0-02 §7.46 (I-19 stop_condition) and D2.0-07 §3 define approval timeout. PART2 correctly derives S3a from approval flow.

ipc_timeout.match: partial — PART2 uses 30s for RPC timeout vs B4 10s base. FIX-027 annotation acknowledges this.

details.description: All 6 state timeouts (S1=5s, S2=30s, S3=120s, S4=10s, S5=15s) in PART2 exactly match D2.0-02 §12.1. S3a=600s matches B4 approval.timeout_s=600. Retry values (Soft Loop 1x, Hard Loop HITL) match D2.0-02/D2.0-05 AC specs. One partial note on ipc_timeout (FIX-027 annotated) is not an error — it's a documented configuration layering decision.

IMP-D.v8_delta: v8 IMP-D was Config Key Validation (17 errors: 15 value mismatches + 2 missing sections, FAIL). v13 re-scopes IMP-D to Timeouts/Retry verification. Different verification target; new result is PASS for timeout-specific checks.

IMP-E.name: Error Codes

warnings.detail: PART2 references failure_code and FailureReport schema but does not enumerate individual error codes inline. It defers to D2.0-02 §6.2 Failure Registry (SOT) which defines 16 confirmed codes for I-1~I-5. This is acceptable as PART2 is an implementation guide, not the error code SOT.

details.sot_additional: OC_ERR_FC_TIMEOUT (D2.0-02 line 587), OC_ERR_SCHEMA_INVALID (D2.0-02 line 577), QUEUE_OVERFLOW (D2.0-02 line 336)

part2_references.failure_report_schema: FailureReport with failure_code(str), message(str), context(dict), occurred_at(datetime) — PART2 line 739, matches D2.1-D5
part2_references.error_handling_pattern: PART2 line 968: JSON-RPC error object (code + message + data). Line 984: test case for unknown method error.
part2_references.i20_failure_manager: PART2 line 1159-1171: I-20 Failure/Fallback Manager generates FailureReport with failure_code reference.

cm08_cross_check.description: CM08 references cross-match shows 234 SINGLE_SOURCE, 5 CONSISTENT, 2 INCONSISTENT (WARNING severity), 0 CRITICAL. The 2 inconsistencies are WARNING-level only, not related to error codes.

details.description: PART2 correctly references the FailureReport schema (4 fields) and defers error code enumeration to D2.0-02 §6.2 SOT (17 confirmed codes for I-1~I-5 + 3 general codes). The VamosError standard (failure_code, message, fallback_id, trace_id) is defined in D2.0-02 §0.3 and referenced in PART2's error handling patterns. CM08 cross-match confirms no CRITICAL reference integrity issues.

IMP-E.v8_delta: v8 IMP-E was Module Activation Matrix (V0-V3 row sums, PASS). v13 re-scopes IMP-E to Error Codes reference integrity. Different verification target; new result is PASS.

IMP-F.name: Test Coverage

warnings.detail: V2 COND module tests (~30) and V3 EXP module tests (~13) are planned in PART2 §6.3 but not yet implementable (future versions). This is expected.

modules_with_test_specs.e2e: 4 test files (chat, cost_limit, approval, error_recovery) — B5 §4

ac_coverage.description: All 50 ACs mapped to at least 1 test case (B5 §7.3 matrix). 100% AC coverage.

details.part2_test_summary: PART2 §6.3 defines ~128 base test items + 190 new items (V1+3, V2+175, V3+12). V0-V1 base: ~85 tests across 5 categories. V2 COND: ~30. V3 EXP: ~13. VAL-001~VAL-010 validation rules defined.
details.description: All modules have test specifications in B5. Every module directory (schemas/, orange_core/, blue_nodes/, safety/, storage/, agent/) has corresponding test files. 50 ACs are 100% mapped to 79 test cases. PART2 §6.3 defines comprehensive test plan with coverage targets. No modules lack test specifications.

IMP-F.v8_delta: v8 IMP-F was Tech Stack Conflict (5 errors: premature Qdrant/Neo4j/S3 references, FAIL). v13 re-scopes IMP-F to Test Coverage verification. Different verification target; new result is PASS.

v8_comparison.note: v8 IMP-A through IMP-F had DIFFERENT verification targets than v13's task specification. v8 scripts verified: IMP-A=Dependency Graph, IMP-B=Schema Field Counts, IMP-C=API Endpoints, IMP-D=Config Keys, IMP-E=Activation Matrix, IMP-F=Tech Stack Conflicts. v13 re-scopes to: IMP-A=File Paths, IMP-B=Dependency Order, IMP-C=Units/Formats, IMP-D=Timeouts, IMP-E=Error Codes, IMP-F=Test Coverage.

scripts_changed.change: Different target (Dependency Graph -> File Paths). Both PASS.
Different target (Schema Fields -> Dependency Order). Both PASS.
Different target (API Endpoints -> Units/Formats). v8 FAIL -> v13 PASS due to different scope.
Different target (Config Keys -> Timeouts). v8 had 17 config value mismatches. v13 timeout-specific checks all pass.
Different target (Activation Matrix -> Error Codes). Both PASS.
Different target (Tech Stack Conflict -> Test Coverage). v8 had 5 premature tech refs. v13 test coverage checks all pass.
